chapter_title.ar: باب الْجَبَائِرِ وَ الْقُرُوحِ وَ الْجِرَاحَاتِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 21 – The splints, and the sores, and the wounds

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/108)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/108)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَثَرْتُ فَانْقَطَعَ ظُفُرِي فَجَعَلْتُ عَلَى إِصْبَعِي مَرَارَةً فَكَيْفَ أَصْنَعُ بِالْوُضُوءِ قَالَ يُعْرَفُ هَذَا وَ أَشْبَاهُهُ مِنْ كِتَابِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ما جَعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ مِنْ حَرَجٍ امْسَحْ عَلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ali Bin Al Hassan Bin Rabaat, from Abdul A’ala, a slave of the family of Saam who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘I stumbled, and my nail got cut. So I made ointment to be upon my toe. How should I deal with the Ablution (Wudhu)?’ Heasws said: ‘Recognise this and it’s like from the Book of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [22:78] He has not Laid upon you a hardship in the Religion. Wipe over it’.
en.sarwar: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Mahbub from Ali ibn al-Hassan ibn Ribat from ‘Abd al-A’la’, Mawla’ Ale Sam who has said the following: “I once said to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘I slipped and my toe nail suffered a cut. I have placed a bitter ointment on my toe. How should I take Wudu’?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘This and such matters are learned from the book of Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, which says, “Allah has not sanctioned anything in religion which may cause you hardships.” You must wipe over the bandage.’””
